question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the signatureFileReader.read(s) call at line 216 by supplying a malformed signature file that causes the reader to return a StreamFileSignature with null fileHash and null fileHashSignature, so that when NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature() checks for null at line 61 of NodeSignatureVerifier.java it returns false but the signature is still added to the multimap, potentially contributing to a false consensus count?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename).block() at line 335 to return null (bypassing the Objects.requireNonNull check by throwing NullPointerException), so that the exception is caught at line 373 and execution continues to the next signature, causing the downloader to silently skip a data file and potentially accept a fraudulent replacement from the next node, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that streamFileReader.read(streamFileData) at line 336 is called before verify() at line 338, so that a maliciously crafted stream file that parses successfully but contains tampered transaction data passes the read phase, and if verify() has a bypass condition (e.g., null hash), the tampered data is passed to onVerified() and persisted, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause streamFileNotifier.verified(streamFile) at line 387 to be called with a stream file that has had its bytes set to null by the isPersistBytes() check at line 351-353, so that downstream processors that depend on the bytes field receive a null value and either crash or process an empty file, leading to missing transaction records and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the parallel Flux processing at line 224 (Schedulers.parallel()) where multiple nodes' signature files are processed concurrently, causing a race condition in the TreeMultimap accumulation at line 223, so that signatures are associated with incorrect filenames and the wrong data files are downloaded and verified, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStreamFileSignatureMultiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the STREAM_FILE_SIGNATURE_COMPARATOR at lines 66-75 which uses Ordering.arbitrary() for value ordering, so that the random ordering of signatures within the same filename group causes non-deterministic selection of which node's data file is downloaded first in verifySignatures(), potentially favoring a fraudulent node's file and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream file where streamFile.getPreviousHash() returns a string that contentEquals() the expectedPreviousHash at line 447 due to string interning or encoding differences, while the actual cryptographic content differs, so that a file with a subtly different previous hash passes the chain verification and a gap in the hash chain goes undetected, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the neededStake calculation at lines 509-512 to overflow a long value when totalStake is near Long.MAX_VALUE and downloadRatio is close to 1.0, so that neededStake wraps to a negative value and the while loop at line 515 terminates immediately with lastEntry=0, causing an empty node collection to be returned and all signature verification to be skipped, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user cause nodes.get(lastEntry++) at line 516 to throw an IndexOutOfBoundsException when aggregateStake never reaches neededStake (e.g., all nodes have 0 stake), causing the entire downloadNextBatch() to fail silently via the catch at line 167, preventing any stream files from being processed and causing direct loss of funds through ledger unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: statusMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the removal of CONSENSUS_REACHED entries from statusMap at line 476 for logging purposes, so that a successful but fraudulent consensus event is never logged, making forensic analysis impossible and allowing an attacker to repeatedly inject fraudulent stream files without detection, causing sustained direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a SignatureVerificationException to propagate from verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() at line 165, which is caught and only logged as a warning (not an error), so that repeated signature verification failures are treated as non-critical events and the downloader continues processing subsequent batches without alerting operators, masking an ongoing attack that causes direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Utility.archiveFile() calls at lines 343 and 347-349 by supplying a stream file with a crafted archiveDestinationFolder path (via importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()) that contains path traversal sequences, so that legitimate stream files are overwritten with fraudulent ones in the archive, causing direct loss of funds through corrupted historical records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the signatures.forEach() at line 347 to archive signature files from nodes that did not reach CONSENSUS_REACHED status, so that fraudulent signature files are archived alongside legitimate ones, corrupting the audit trail and making it impossible to detect that a fraudulent consensus was achieved, leading to undetected direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the streamFileProvider.list(node, startAfterFilename) call at line 213 by injecting a crafted startAfterFilename (via manipulation of lastStreamFile) that causes the provider to list files from an earlier point in time, so that already-processed stream files are re-downloaded and re-ingested, causing duplicate transaction records and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream file where getFileHash() returns an empty string and the signature's getFileHashAsHex() also returns an empty string, so that Objects.equals('', '') at line 432 passes the file hash check, allowing a file with no meaningful hash to be accepted as verified and causing direct loss of funds through ingestion of tampered transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, streamFile.getConsensusEnd()) at line 395 to produce an Instant far in the future by supplying a stream file with a manipulated consensusEnd value, so that cloudStorageLatencyMetric records a negative duration, corrupting monitoring data and masking the fact that a fraudulent file was injected with a future timestamp, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ShutdownHelper.isStopping() check at line 284 by triggering a shutdown signal at a precise moment when a legitimate batch is being processed but a fraudulent batch has already been queued, so that the legitimate batch is skipped and the fraudulent batch is processed in the next cycle, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the .block() call at line 225 to block indefinitely if the Flux never completes (e.g., due to a hanging streamFileProvider.list() call), causing the entire importer thread to deadlock and preventing any stream files from being processed, leading to direct loss of funds through ledger unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that signature.getDataFilename() at line 332 derives the data filename by replacing SIGNATURE_SUFFIX in the signature filename, so that a crafted signature filename where SIGNATURE_SUFFIX appears multiple times causes the wrong data filename to be constructed, leading to the wrong data file being downloaded and verified, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause signature.getNode() at line 333 to return a node object with a different nodeId than the one that signed the signature file, exploiting a mismatch between the node used for signature verification and the node used for data file download, so that a fraudulent data file from a different node is accepted as verified, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly trigger the generic Exception catch at line 167 by causing streamFileProvider operations to throw unexpected exceptions, so that the error is logged but the lastStreamFile pointer is never advanced, causing the downloader to repeatedly attempt and fail to process the same batch, effectively halting ledger updates and causing direct loss of funds through stale state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user cause consensusNodeService.getNodes() to return a list where all nodes have stake=0 except one colluding node, so that partialCollection() always selects only the colluding node (since its stake alone meets neededStake=0), allowing that single node to achieve CONSENSUS_REACHED and inject fraudulent stream files, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sigFilesMap with a very large number of filename groups, so that the for-loop at line 283 processes them all sequentially and the earliestFilename variable at line 281 is set to the first (potentially fraudulent) group, causing the warning message at line 367 to reference an incorrect earliest failure point and masking the true scope of an attack, leading to undetected direct loss of funds?
